arabic: إنما الأعمال بالنيات وإنما لكل امرئ ما نوى
english: Actions are judged by intentions, and everyone will get what they intended
subtitle: On Intentions
tafseer: This foundational hadith teaches that the value of our deeds depends on our intentions. External appearances mean nothing without sincere internal purpose. It emphasizes that our intentions must be pure and directed toward pleasing Allah.

arabic: الدين النصيحة
english: Religion is sincere advice to Allah, His Messenger, and Muslims
subtitle: On Religion
tafseer: This hadith defines Islam as giving sincere counsel. A Muslim should advise others toward righteousness, correct mistakes with compassion, and maintain integrity in all relationships with the community.

arabic: من سنّ سنة حسنة فعمل بها بعده كان له أجره وأجر من عمل بها
english: Whoever establishes a good practice, and people act upon it, will have their reward and the reward of those who act upon it
subtitle: On Good Practices
tafseer: This hadith encourages positive innovation in Islamic practice. When we establish good customs and others follow them, we continue to earn reward through their deeds. It motivates us to set good examples.

arabic: الراحمون يرحمهم الرحمن ارحم من في الأرض يرحمك من في السماء
english: The merciful will be shown mercy by the Most Merciful. Be merciful to those on earth
subtitle: On Mercy
tafseer: This hadith establishes the principle of reciprocal mercy. Allah's mercy is earned through our compassion toward others. It teaches that treating people with kindness results in Allah's compassion toward us.

arabic: لا يؤمن أحدكم حتى يحب لأخيه ما يحب لنفسه
english: None of you believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself
subtitle: On Brotherhood
tafseer: This hadith defines true faith as genuine care for others. Real belief requires us to want good for others as we want it for ourselves, indicating empathy and unity in the Muslim community.

arabic: من لا يشكر الناس لا يشكر الله
english: Whoever does not thank people has not thanked Allah
subtitle: On Gratitude
tafseer: Gratitude to people is an expression of gratitude to Allah. This hadith teaches that acknowledging others' kindness reflects appreciation for Allah's blessings through them.

arabic: الكلمة الطيبة صدقة
english: A kind word is an act of charity
subtitle: On Kind Speech
tafseer: This hadith elevates the value of good words. Speaking kindly to others, encouraging them, or offering wise counsel counts as charity. It shows that our words have charitable value.

arabic: خيركم خيركم لأهله وأنا خيركم لأهلي
english: The best among you are those who are best to their families, and I am the best to my family
subtitle: On Family
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes that excellence in faith is demonstrated through family relations. How we treat our spouses and families is a measure of our character and devotion to Islam.

arabic: اتق الله حيثما كنت وأتبع السيئة الحسنة تمحها
english: Fear Allah wherever you are, and follow a bad deed with a good one to erase it
subtitle: On Piety
tafseer: This hadith encourages constant awareness of Allah and personal accountability. It teaches that good deeds can compensate for mistakes and that we should strive for improvement.

arabic: من غدا إلى المسجد أو راح أعدّ الله له ضيافة في الجنة
english: Whoever goes to the mosque in the morning or evening, Allah prepares for him a place in Paradise
subtitle: On Mosques
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the spiritual reward of regular mosque attendance. Visiting the mosque for prayer is rewarded with Paradise, showing Allah's appreciation for our devotion.

arabic: رحم الله امرأ قام من الليل فصلى وأيقظ امرأته فإن أبت نضح في وجهها الماء
english: May Allah have mercy on a man who wakes up at night to pray and awakens his wife
subtitle: On Night Prayer
tafseer: This hadith encourages night prayer and sharing spiritual practices with one's spouse. It shows the value of Tahajjud prayer and mutual spiritual development in marriage.

arabic: الحج عرفة
english: Hajj is Arafat (standing at Mount Arafat)
subtitle: On Hajj
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the importance of standing at Mount Arafat, the central ritual of pilgrimage. It symbolizes the climax of Hajj where forgiveness and mercy are most abundant.

arabic: إن الله يحب إذا عمل أحدكم عملا أن يتقنه
english: Allah loves that when any of you does something, he does it with excellence
subtitle: On Excellence
tafseer: This hadith teaches the Islamic principle of Ihsan (excellence). Whether work is religious or worldly, doing it well is an act of worship and pleases Allah.

arabic: المسلم أخو المسلم لا يظلمه ولا يخذله ولا يحقره
english: A Muslim is the brother of another Muslim: he does not oppress him nor does he abandon him
subtitle: On Brotherhood
tafseer: This hadith defines the bond of Islamic brotherhood as one of mutual respect and support. Muslims should not harm, betray, or demean each other.

arabic: بينما نحن عند النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم إذ جاء رجل يسعى
english: While we were sitting with the Prophet, a man came running
subtitle: On Respect
tafseer: This hadith introduction emphasizes respectful conduct in gatherings of Islamic knowledge. Proper decorum and reverence for sacred settings is important.

arabic: من قطع رحمه دخل النار ولا تدخل الجنة نفس قاطعة الرحم
english: Whoever severs the ties of kinship will not enter Paradise
subtitle: On Kinship
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the importance of maintaining family bonds. Cutting off relations with family members is among the grave sins that prevent entry to Paradise.

arabic: أفضل الزكاة ما كانت عند غنى وابدأ بمن تعول
english: The best charity is when given despite need, and begin with those you support
subtitle: On Charity
tafseer: This hadith teaches that the most virtuous charity comes from limited means, and helping one's dependents takes priority. Quality over quantity defines great charity.

arabic: سلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته هذه تحية الله إياكم
english: Peace be upon you and the mercy and blessings of Allah - this is Allah's greeting
subtitle: On Greetings
tafseer: This hadith teaches the Islamic greeting and its spiritual significance. Saying Assalamu alaikum is more than courtesy; it's a supplication for divine peace and blessings.

arabic: من آمن بالله واليوم الآخر فليكرم ضيفه
english: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should honor his guest
subtitle: On Hospitality
tafseer: This hadith links hospitality to faith. Treating guests with honor and generosity is a demonstration of Islamic belief and a path to Paradise.

arabic: الصائم في الحج والعمرة كالشاة في الحرم
english: A fasting pilgrim is like a slaughtered animal in the Haram
subtitle: On Fasting During Hajj
tafseer: This hadith advises against fasting while in Ihram (state of pilgrimage). It emphasizes that certain practices are wisely timed for spiritual and physical well-being.

arabic: لا يستقيم إيمان عبد حتى يستقيم قلبه ولا يستقيم قلبه حتى يستقيم لسانه
english: A person's faith is not complete until their heart is sound, and their heart cannot be sound until their tongue is sound
subtitle: On The Heart and Tongue
tafseer: This hadith teaches that true faith requires purity of the heart and honesty in speech. Our words reveal the state of our hearts, and both must align with Islamic values.

arabic: من ستر مسلما ستره الله يوم القيامة
english: Whoever conceals the fault of a Muslim, Allah will conceal his faults on the Day of Judgment
subtitle: On Concealing Faults
tafseer: This hadith teaches discretion and mercy in handling others' mistakes. Protecting someone's reputation through silence is rewarded by Allah protecting our own.

arabic: إذا مرضت فهو يشفين وإذا أنا جعت فهو يطعمين
english: When I am ill, He cures me, and when I am hungry, He feeds me
subtitle: On Allah's Care
tafseer: This hadith expresses perfect trust in Allah's provision. It teaches believers to look to Allah as the healer and sustainer in all circumstances.

arabic: من يرد الله به خيرا يصب منه
english: Whoever Allah wants good for, He tests them
subtitle: On Tests
tafseer: This hadith places trials in a positive light. Tests and challenges are signs of Allah's care and opportunities for spiritual growth and purification.

arabic: ما من مسلم يموت فيقوم على جنازته صف من المسلمين ما يقل عددهم ثلاثة فيصلون عليه
english: No Muslim dies such that the Muslim standover his funeral prayer being at least three rows
subtitle: On Funeral Prayers
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the communal responsibility to attend funeral prayers. Community support in death is a manifestation of brotherhood.

arabic: الحياء شعبة من الإيمان والخيلاء شعبة من النفاق
english: Modesty is a part of faith, and vain glory is a part of hypocrisy
subtitle: On Modesty
tafseer: This hadith distinguishes between healthy shame that guides us toward good and arrogance that separates us from Allah. True faith includes appropriate humility.

arabic: من بدّل دينه فاقتلوه
english: Whoever changes his faith, execute him
subtitle: On Apostasy
tafseer: This hadith addresses apostasy as a serious matter. It emphasizes the gravity of abandoning Islam deliberately, though context and conditions apply in Islamic jurisprudence.

arabic: الإيمان بضع وسبعون شعبة أعلاها لا إله إلا الله وأدناها إماطة الأذى عن الطريق
english: Faith has more than seventy branches, the highest is 'La ilaha illallah' and the lowest is removing harm from the path
subtitle: On Faith
tafseer: This hadith teaches that faith encompasses many practices, from core beliefs to simple acts of kindness. All contribute to a complete Islamic practice.

arabic: الجار قبل الدار والرفيق قبل الطريق
english: Know your neighbor before you buy a house, and know your companion before you start the journey
subtitle: On Relationships
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the importance of good relationships and wise choices in companions. Our environment shapes our character and well-being.

arabic: أد الأمانة إلى من ائتمنك ولا تخن من خانك
english: Return the trust to one who entrusted you, and do not betray one who betrayed you
subtitle: On Trusts
tafseer: This hadith teaches moral integrity and honesty. Fulfilling trust is a sacred duty, and we should not compromise our principles even if others do.

arabic: اجتنب السجود على سبعة
english: Avoid prostration on seven parts
subtitle: On Prayer
tafseer: This hadith provides guidance on proper prayer posture, teaching that our bodies should show reverence during worship. Physical form in prayer reflects spiritual devotion.

arabic: من قال حين يصبح وحين يمسي اللهم إني أسلمت نفسي إليك
english: Whoever says in the morning and evening: O Allah, I submit myself to You
subtitle: On Remembrance
tafseer: This hadith teaching shows the value of regular morning and evening invocations. Daily remembrance keeps Allah in our consciousness and strengthens our submission.

arabic: إن الله لا ينظر إلى أجسادكم ولا إلى صوركم ولكن ينظر إلى قلوبكم
english: Allah does not look at your bodies or forms, but looks at your hearts
subtitle: On Hearts
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the primacy of the heart in Islamic practice. Physical beauty and wealth mean nothing; sincere belief and righteous intentions matter.

arabic: الحسب واللون والجمال لا يورثان والعقل والعلم والأدب يورثان
english: Lineage, color, and beauty are not inherited, but intellect, knowledge, and manners are
subtitle: On Inheritance
tafseer: This hadith teaches the value of cultivating character and knowledge. True legacy comes from virtues and learning passed to future generations.

arabic: من تعلم لغة قوم أمن مكرهم
english: Whoever learns the language of a people is safe from their deception
subtitle: On Languages
tafseer: This hadith encourages learning and cultural understanding. Knowledge empowers us and bridges divides, leading to better understanding and peace.

arabic: قل لا أملك لنفسي نفعا ولا ضرا إلا ما شاء الله
english: Say: I have not control over any benefit or harm for myself except as Allah wills
subtitle: On Divine Will
tafseer: This hadith reminds us of our complete dependence on Allah. We should seek His will while doing our best, acknowledging that ultimate control rests with Him.

arabic: لا تغضبوا فإن الغضب من الشيطان
english: Do not become angry, for anger is from the devil
subtitle: On Anger
tafseer: This hadith warns against the destructive nature of anger. Controlling our temper is essential to resisting Satan's influence and maintaining spiritual peace.

arabic: آمن الجار بالجار والدار بالدار والرفيق بالرفيق
english: Believe in neighbors by neighbors, and homes by homes, and companions by companions
subtitle: On Community
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the interdependence of community members. Neighborly relations and companionship are built on mutual trust and shared responsibility.

arabic: من عزل نفسه عن قومه أشفق الناس عليه
english: Whoever isolates himself from his people, people become sympathetic to him
subtitle: On Isolation
tafseer: This hadith teaches that withdrawing from society may lead to pity rather than respect. Engagement with community is valued in Islamic culture.

arabic: من بات طاهرا بات ملك في فراشه
english: Whoever goes to sleep in a state of purity, an angel stays in his bed
subtitle: On Purity
tafseer: This hadith teaches that maintaining ritual purity before sleep brings divine blessing. It encourages cleanliness and spiritual preparation before resting.

arabic: لا تحقرن من المعروف شيئا ولو أن تلقى أخاك بوجه طليق
english: Do not despise any good deed, even if it is to greet your brother with a smiling face
subtitle: On Good Deeds
tafseer: This hadith teaches that small kindnesses have great value. A smile is charity; every gesture of warmth matters and should not be underestimated.

arabic: العقل غريزة تزيد بالعلم والتجارب
english: Intellect is an instinct that increases with knowledge and experience
subtitle: On Intelligence
tafseer: This hadith teaches that intelligence grows through seeking knowledge and learning from experiences. Continuous learning elevates our understanding and wisdom.

arabic: من تتبع عورات الناس تتبع الله عورته
english: Whoever seeks the faults of people, Allah will seek his faults
subtitle: On Privacy
tafseer: This hadith warns against gossip and spreading rumors. Exposing others' mistakes invites exposure of our own; justice and mercy demand discretion.

arabic: احذر من الفاسد والفسد والفاسق والفسيق
english: Beware of the corrupt, corruption, the corrupt person and the corrupt action
subtitle: On Corruption
tafseer: This hadith warns against all forms of corruption. We must avoid corrupt people and practices to maintain our spiritual integrity.

arabic: من قال لا إله إلا الله دخل الجنة
english: Whoever says 'There is no god but Allah' enters Paradise
subtitle: On Tawheed
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes the power of sincere belief in monotheism. Genuine utterance of the Shahada with conviction is a path to eternal reward.

arabic: السفر قطعة من العذاب
english: Traveling is a piece of punishment
subtitle: On Travel
tafseer: This hadith acknowledges the hardships of travel. It teaches patience during journeys and reminds us that discomforts bring spiritual reward.

arabic: ما أحد أعظم جرما عند الله من رجل تولى أمر أمة فلم يحطط عليهم
english: No one commits a greater sin before Allah than a person appointed over people who neglects them
subtitle: On Leadership
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes accountability of leaders. Those in authority bear responsibility for their people's welfare; neglect is among the gravest sins.

arabic: من جلس في مجلس يلغو فيه سمع ناشرة من النار
english: Whoever sits in a gathering where vain talk occurs hears a blast from Hell
subtitle: On Gatherings
tafseer: This hadith warns against idle gatherings with meaningless talk. We should seek beneficial knowledge and meaningful conversation.

arabic: كل الناس يصبح فيبيع نفسه فمنهم من يعتقها ومنهم من يوبقها
english: Every person sets out to sell himself in the morning, some free themselves and some enslave themselves
subtitle: On Actions
tafseer: This hadith teaches personal accountability. Each day we choose to elevate our soul through good deeds or enslave it through sin.

arabic: من حسن إسلام المرء تركه ما لا يعنيه
english: From the signs of a good Muslim is that he abandons what does not concern him
subtitle: On Focus
tafseer: This hadith teaches discretion and focus. A mature Muslim concerns himself with matters that matter and avoids gossip and frivolous matters.

arabic: الحيل والحرام في جهنم
english: Tricks and tricks to evade Islamic law lead to Hell
subtitle: On Honesty
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes integrity and honesty in transactions. Using deception to circumvent Islamic principles is prohibited and leads to punishment.

arabic: احفظ الله يحفظك احفظ الله تجده تجاهك
english: Keep Allah's commands, He will keep you. Remember Allah, He will remember you
subtitle: On Protection
tafseer: This hadith teaches reciprocity in our relationship with Allah. Obedience and remembrance of Allah bring His protection and care.

arabic: من رأى منكم منكرا فليغيره بيده فإن لم يستطع فبلسانه فإن لم يستطع ففي قلبه
english: Whoever sees an evil must change it with his hand, or if unable then with his tongue, or if unable then with his heart
subtitle: On Changing Evil
tafseer: This hadith outlines the levels of fighting against evil. From direct action to gentle reminders to internal disapproval, we all have a role in upholding justice.

arabic: لولا ستر الله علينا لفضحنا ولولا رحمة الله إلى أين يذهب بنا
english: If Allah did not conceal our faults, He would expose us; if not for His mercy, where would we go
subtitle: On Allah's Mercy
tafseer: This hadith reflects gratitude for Allah's concealment of our sins. It teaches humility and appreciation for divine mercy that sustains us.

arabic: ما من أحد ينام إلا وملك يحرسه
english: There is no one who sleeps except that an angel guards him
subtitle: On Angels
tafseer: This hadith provides comfort and security. Angels watch over us while we sleep, offering divine protection and care during our vulnerable hours.

arabic: إذا قام أحدكم يصلي فلا يبصق قبل وجهه ولا عن يمينه
english: When any of you stands to pray, do not spit in front of you or to your right
subtitle: On Prayer
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes respect during prayer. Our comportment during worship reflects reverence for Allah and respect for the sacred act of prayer.

arabic: الطيرة شرك
english: Ill omens are a form of shirk (polytheism)
subtitle: On Superstition
tafseer: This hadith rejects superstition and fortune-telling. Relying on omens instead of Allah is a form of polytheism that destroys faith in divine provision.

arabic: والله لا يؤمن والله لا يؤمن والله لا يؤمن، قيل يا رسول الله من؟ قال من لا يأمن جاره بوائقه
english: By Allah, he does not believe, by Allah he does not believe - it was asked who O Messenger - the one whom his neighbor is not safe from his harm
subtitle: On Neighbors
tafseer: This hadith emphasizes that genuine faith includes safety and respect for neighbors. Harming neighbors is a serious violation of Islamic principles.

arabic: إن الله لا ينسى أحدا لكن الناس ينسون
english: Allah does not forget anyone, but people forget
subtitle: On Memory
tafseer: This hadith reminds us that Allah remembers all our deeds and intentions. Our eternal account is never forgotten by our Lord despite human forgetfulness.
